commands.name: service
commands.description:
Service lifecycle management: start, stop, 
status.

commands.name: start
commands.description:
Start the ExcelMCP Service if not already 
running.

commands.name: stop
commands.description: Gracefully stop the ExcelMCP Service.

commands.name: status
commands.description:
Show service status (running, PID, sessions, 
uptime).

commands.name: batch

options.name: --input
options.description:
JSON file with command array. Use '-' for stdin (NDJSON, 
one command per line). If omitted, reads from stdin.

options.name: --session
options.description:
Default session ID for all commands. Overridden by 
per-command sessionId. Auto-captured from session.open/create if not 
set.

options.name: --stop-on-error
options.description:
Stop execution on first error (default: continue all 
commands).

commands.description:
Execute multiple commands from a JSON file or stdin. Outputs 
NDJSON (one result per line).

commands.name: session
commands.description:
Session management. WORKFLOW: open -> use sessionId -> 
close (--save to persist).

commands.name: create

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Session timeout in seconds

arguments.name: FILE
arguments.description: Path to the new Excel file to create

commands.description:
Create a new Excel file, open it, and create a 
session.

commands.name: open

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Session timeout in seconds

arguments.name: FILE
arguments.description: Path to the Excel file to open

commands.description: Open an Excel file and create a session.

commands.name: close

options.name: --save
options.description: Save changes before closing

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID to close

commands.description: Close a session. Use --save to persist changes.

commands.name: list
commands.description: List active sessions.

commands.name: save

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID to save

commands.description: Save a session without closing it.

commands.name: calculationmode

options.name: --mode
options.description:
Target calculation mode (required for: 
set-mode)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --range-address
options.description: Range address (required for Range scope)

options.name: --scope
options.description:
Scope: Workbook, Sheet, or Range (required for: 
calculate)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description: Sheet name (required for Sheet/Range scope)

commands.description:
Control Excel recalculation (automatic vs manual). Set manual 
mode before bulk writes for faster performance, then recalculate once at the 
end.

commands.name: chart

options.name: --chart-name
options.description:
Name of the chart (or shape name) (required for: read, 
delete, move, fit-to-range)

options.name: --chart-type
options.description:
Type of chart to create (required for: create-from-range, 
create-from-table, create-from-pivottable)

options.name: --height
options.description: Chart height in points

options.name: --left
options.description: Left position in points from worksheet edge

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --pivot-table-name
options.description:
Name of the source PivotTable (required for: 
create-from-pivottable)

options.name: --range-address
options.description:
Range to fit the chart to (e.g., A1:D10) (required for: 
fit-to-range)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description:
Target worksheet name (required for: create-from-range, 
create-from-table, create-from-pivottable, fit-to-range)

options.name: --source-range-address
options.description:
Data range for the chart (e.g., A1:D10) (required for: 
create-from-range)

options.name: --table-name
options.description:
Name of the Excel Table (required for: 
create-from-table)

options.name: --target-range
options.description:
Cell range to position chart within (e.g., 'F2:K15'). 
PREFERRED over left/top. When set, left/top are ignored.

options.name: --top
options.description: Top position in points from worksheet edge

options.name: --width
options.description: Chart width in points

commands.description:
Chart lifecycle - create, read, move, and delete embedded 
charts. POSITIONING (choose one): - targetRange (PREFERRED): Cell range like 
'F2:K15' — positions chart within cells, no point math needed. - left/top: 
Manual positioning in points (72 points = 1 inch). - Neither: Auto-positions 
chart below all existing content (used range + other charts). COLLISION 
DETECTION: All create/move/fit-to-range operations automatically check for 
overlaps with data and other charts. Warnings are returned in the result message
if collisions are detected. Always verify layout with screenshot(capture-sheet) 
after creating charts. CHART TYPES: 70+ types available including Column, Line, 
Pie, Bar, Area, XY Scatter. CREATE OPTIONS: - create-from-range: Create from 
cell range (e.g., 'A1:D10') - create-from-table: Create from Excel Table (uses 
table's data range) - create-from-pivottable: Create linked PivotChart Use 
chartconfig for series, titles, legends, styles, placement mode.

commands.name: chartconfig

options.name: --axis

Which axis to set title for (Category, Value, SeriesAxis) 
(required for: set-axis-title, get-axis-number-format, set-axis-number-format, 
get-axis-scale, set-axis-scale, set-gridlines)

options.name: --chart-type

Position of data labels relative to data 
points

options.name: --legend-position

options.name: --marker-foreground-color

options.name: --marker-size
options.description: Marker size in points (2-72)

options.name: --marker-style
options.description: Marker shape style

options.name: --maximum-scale
options.description: Maximum axis value (null for auto)

options.name: --series-name
options.description:
Display name for the series (required for: 
add-series)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --show-bubble-size
options.description: Show bubble size (bubble charts)

options.name: --show-category-name
options.description: Show category name on labels

options.name: --show-major
options.description: Show major gridlines (null to keep current)

options.name: --show-minor
options.description: Show minor gridlines (null to keep current)

options.name: --show-percentage
options.description:
Show percentage values. Only meaningful for pie and 
doughnut chart types; setting to true on other chart types has no visual 
effect.

options.name: --show-series-name
options.description: Show series name on labels

options.name: --show-value
options.description: Show data values on labels

options.name: --source-range
options.description:
New data source range (e.g., Sheet1!A1:D10) (required for: 
set-source-range)

options.name: --style-id
options.description:
Excel chart style ID (1-48 for most chart types) (required 
for: set-style)

options.name: --title
options.description:
Title text to display (required for: set-title, 
set-axis-title)

options.name: --trendline-index
options.description:
1-based index of the trendline to delete (required for: 
delete-trendline, set-trendline)

options.name: --trendline-type
options.description:
Type of trendline (Linear, Exponential, etc.) (required 
for: add-trendline)

options.name: --values-range
options.description:
Range containing series values (e.g., B2:B10) (required 
for: add-series)

options.name: --visible
options.description:
True to show legend, false to hide (required for: 
show-legend)

commands.description:
Chart configuration - data source, series, type, title, axis 
labels, legend, and styling. SERIES MANAGEMENT: - add-series: Add data series 
with valuesRange (required) and optional categoryRange - remove-series: Remove 
series by 1-based index - set-source-range: Replace entire chart data source 
TITLES AND LABELS: - set-title: Set chart title (empty string hides title) - 
set-axis-title: Set axis labels (Category, Value, CategorySecondary, 
ValueSecondary) CHART STYLES: 1-48 (built-in Excel styles with different color 
schemes) DATA LABELS: Show values, percentages, series/category names. 
Positions: Center, InsideEnd, InsideBase, OutsideEnd, BestFit. TRENDLINES: 
Linear, Exponential, Logarithmic, Polynomial (order 2-6), Power, MovingAverage. 
PLACEMENT MODE: - 1: Move and size with cells - 2: Move but don't size with 
cells - 3: Don't move or size with cells (free floating) Use chart for lifecycle
operations (create, delete, move, fit-to-range).

options.description: First formula/value for condition

options.description: Second formula/value (for between/notBetween)

options.name: --interior-color
options.description: Fill color (#RRGGBB or color index)

options.name: --interior-pattern
options.description:
Interior pattern (1=Solid, -4142=None, 9=Gray50,

options.name: --operator-type
options.description:
XlFormatConditionOperator: equal, notEqual, greater, less, 
greaterEqual, lessEqual, between, notBetween

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --range-address
options.description:
Range address (A1 notation or named range) 
(required)

options.name: --rule-type
options.description:
Rule type: cellValue (or cell-value), expression, 
colorScale, dataBar, top10, iconSet, uniqueValues, blanksCondition, timePeriod, 
aboveAverage. Both camelCase and kebab-case accepted. (required for: 
add-rule)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description:
Sheet name (empty for active sheet) 
(required)

commands.description:
Conditional formatting - visual rules based on cell values. 
TYPES: cellValue (requires operatorType+formula1), expression (formula only). 
Both camelCase and kebab-case accepted. FORMAT: interiorColor/fontColor as 
#RRGGBB, fontBold/Italic, borderStyle/Color. OPERATORS: equal, notEqual, 
greater, less, greaterEqual, lessEqual, between, notBetween. For 'between' and 
'notBetween', both formula1 and formula2 are required.

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Optional: Timeout for the refresh operation

commands.description:
Data Model (Power Pivot) - DAX measures and table management. 
CRITICAL: WORKSHEET TABLES AND DATA MODEL ARE SEPARATE! - After table append 
changes, Data Model still has OLD data - MUST call refresh to sync changes - 
Power Query refresh auto-syncs (no manual refresh needed) PREREQUISITE: Tables 
must be added to the Data Model first. Use table add-to-datamodel for worksheet 
tables, or powerquery to import and load data directly to the Data Model. DAX 
MEASURES: - Create with DAX formulas like 'SUM(Sales[[Amount]])' - DAX formulas 
are auto-formatted on CREATE/UPDATE via Dax.Formatter (SQLBI) - Read operations 
return raw DAX as stored DAX EVALUATE QUERIES: - Use evaluate to execute DAX 
EVALUATE queries against the Data Model - Returns tabular results from queries 
like 'EVALUATE TableName' - Supports complex DAX: SUMMARIZE, FILTER, 
CALCULATETABLE, TOPN, etc. DMV (DYNAMIC MANAGEMENT VIEW) QUERIES: - Use 
execute-dmv to query Data Model metadata via SQL-like syntax - Syntax: SELECT * 
FROM $SYSTEM.SchemaRowset (ONLY SELECT * supported) - Use 
DISCOVER_SCHEMA_ROWSETS to list all available DMVs Use datamodelrel for 
relationships between tables.

commands.name: datamodelrelationship

options.name: --active
options.description:
Whether the relationship should be active (default: true) 
(required for: update-relationship)

options.name: --from-column
options.description:
Source column name (required for: read-relationship, 
create-relationship, update-relationship, delete-relationship)

options.name: --from-table
options.description:
Source table name (required for: read-relationship, 
create-relationship, update-relationship, delete-relationship)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --to-column
options.description:
Target column name (required for: read-relationship, 
create-relationship, update-relationship, delete-relationship)

options.name: --to-table
options.description:
Target table name (required for: read-relationship, 
create-relationship, update-relationship, delete-relationship)

commands.description:
Data Model relationships - link tables for cross-table DAX 
calculations. CRITICAL: Deleting or recreating tables removes ALL their 
relationships. Use list-relationships before table operations to backup, then 
recreate relationships after schema changes. RELATIONSHIP REQUIREMENTS: - Both 
tables must exist in the Data Model first - Columns must have compatible data 
types - fromTable/fromColumn = many-side (detail table, foreign key) - 
toTable/toColumn = one-side (lookup table, primary key) ACTIVE VS INACTIVE: - 
Only ONE active relationship can exist between two tables - Use active=false 
when creating alternative paths - DAX USERELATIONSHIP() activates inactive 
relationships

and error handling — no Excel COM session needed.

Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --pivot-table-name
options.description:
Name of the PivotTable (required for: read, 
create-from-range, create-from-table, create-from-datamodel, delete,

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --source-range
options.description:
Source range address (e.g., "A1:F100") (required for:

options.name: --source-sheet
options.description:
Source worksheet name (required for:

Name of the Excel Table (required for: create-from-table,

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Optional timeout for the refresh operation

commands.description:
PivotTable lifecycle management: create from various sources, 
list, read details, refresh, and delete. Use pivottablefield for field 
operations, pivottablecalc for calculated fields and layout. BEST PRACTICE: Use 
'list' before creating. Prefer 'refresh' or field modifications over 
delete+recreate. Delete+recreate loses field configurations, filters, sorting, 
and custom layouts. REFRESH: Call 'refresh' after configuring fields with 
pivottablefield to update the visual display. This is especially important for 
OLAP/Data Model PivotTables where field operations are structural only and don't
automatically trigger a visual refresh. CREATE OPTIONS: - 'create-from-range': 
Use source sheet and range address for data range - 'create-from-table': Use an 
Excel Table (ListObject) as source - 'create-from-datamodel': Use a Power Pivot 
Data Model table as source

(optional)

Name for the calculated field (required for: 
create-calculated-field, delete-calculated-field, set-subtotals)

options.name: --formula
options.description:
Formula using field references (e.g., "=Revenue-Cost") 
(required for: create-calculated-field, create-calculated-member)

options.name: --member-name
options.description:
Name for the calculated member (MDX naming format) 
(required for: create-calculated-member, delete-calculated-member)

options.name: --number-format
options.description:
Number format code for the calculated member 
(optional)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --pivot-table-name
options.description: Name of the PivotTable (required)

options.name: --row-layout
options.description:
Layout form: 0=Compact, 1=Tabular, 2=Outline (required for:
set-layout)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --show-column-grand-totals
options.description:
Show column grand totals (right summary column) (required 
for: set-grand-totals)

options.name: --show-row-grand-totals
options.description:
Show row grand totals (bottom summary row) (required for: 
set-grand-totals)

options.name: --show-subtotals
options.description:
True to show automatic subtotals, false to hide (required 
for: set-subtotals)

options.name: --solve-order
options.description:
Solve order for calculation precedence (default: 
0)

options.name: --type
options.description:
Type of calculated member (Member, Set, or 
Measure)

commands.description:
PivotTable calculated fields/members, layout configuration, and
data extraction. Use pivottable for lifecycle, pivottablefield for field 
placement. CALCULATED FIELDS (for regular PivotTables): - Create custom fields 
using formulas like '=Revenue-Cost' or '=Quantity*UnitPrice' - Can reference 
existing fields by name - After creating, use pivottablefield add-value-field to
add to Values area - For complex multi-table calculations, prefer DAX measures 
with datamodel CALCULATED MEMBERS (for OLAP/Data Model PivotTables only): - 
Create using MDX expressions - Member types: Member, Set, Measure LAYOUT 
OPTIONS: - 0 = Compact (default, fields in single column) - 1 = Tabular (each 
field in separate column - best for export/analysis) - 2 = Outline (hierarchical
with expand/collapse)

options.name: --output

Values to show (others will be hidden) (required for:

options.name: --session

options.name: --start
options.description: Starting value (null = use field minimum)

commands.description:
PivotTable field management: add/remove/configure fields, 
filtering, sorting, and grouping. Use pivottable for lifecycle, pivottablecalc 
for calculated fields and layout. IMPORTANT: Field operations modify structure 
only. Call pivottable refresh after configuring fields to update the visual 
display, especially for OLAP/Data Model PivotTables. FIELD AREAS: - Row fields: 
Group data by categories (add-row-field) - Column fields: Create column headers 
(add-column-field) - Value fields: Aggregate numeric data with Sum, Count, 
Average, etc. (add-value-field) - Filter fields: Add report-level filters 
(add-filter-field) AGGREGATION FUNCTIONS: Sum, Count, Average, Max, Min, 
Product, CountNumbers, StdDev, StdDevP, Var, VarP GROUPING: - Date fields: Group
by Days, Months, Quarters, Years (group-by-date) - Numeric fields: Group by 
ranges with start/end/interval (group-by-numeric) NUMBER FORMAT: Use US format 
codes like '#,##0.00' for currency or '0.00%' for percentages.

commands.name: powerquery

options.name: --load-destination
options.description: Load destination mode (required for: load-to)

options.name: --m-code
options.description:
Raw M code (inline string) (required for: create, update, 
evaluate)

options.name: --m-code-file
options.description: Path to file containing mCode

options.name: --new-name
options.description: New name for the query (required for: rename)

options.name: --old-name
options.description:
Current name of the query (required for: 
rename)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --query-name
options.description:
Name of the query to view (required for: view, refresh, 
get-load-config, delete, create, update, load-to, unload)

options.name: --refresh
options.description:
Whether to refresh data after update (default: 
true)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --target-cell-address
options.description:
Optional target cell address for worksheet loads (e.g., 
"B5"). Required when loading to an existing worksheet with other 
data.

options.name: --target-sheet
options.description:
Target worksheet name (required for LoadToTable and 
LoadToBoth; defaults to query name when omitted)

options.name: --timeout
options.description:
Maximum time to wait for refresh (required for: 
refresh)

commands.description:
Power Query M code and data loading. TEST-FIRST DEVELOPMENT 
WORKFLOW (BEST PRACTICE): 1. evaluate - Test M code WITHOUT persisting (catches 
syntax errors, validates sources, shows data preview) 2. create/update - Store 
VALIDATED query in workbook 3. refresh/load-to - Load data to destination Skip 
evaluate only for trivial literal tables. IF CREATE/UPDATE FAILS: Use evaluate 
to get the actual M engine error message, fix code, retry. DATETIME COLUMNS: 
Always include Table.TransformColumnTypes() in M code to set column types 
explicitly. Without explicit types, dates may be stored as numbers and Data 
Model relationships may fail. DESTINATIONS: 'worksheet' (default), 'data-model' 
(for DAX), 'both', 'connection-only'. Use 'data-model' to load to Power Pivot, 
then use datamodel to create DAX measures. TARGET CELL: targetCellAddress places
tables without clearing sheet. TIMEOUT: 30 min auto-timeout for refresh and 
load-to. For quick queries, use timeout=60 or similar. timeout=0 or omitted uses
the 30 min default.

commands.name: range

options.name: --cell-address
options.description:
Single cell address (e.g., 'B5') - expands to contiguous 
data region around this cell (required for: get-current-region)

options.name: --format-code
options.description:
Number format code in US locale (e.g., '#,##0.00' for 
numbers, 'mm/dd/yyyy' for dates, '0.00%' for percentages, 'General' for default,
'@' for text) (required for: set-number-format)

options.name: --formats
options.description:
2D array of format codes - same dimensions as target range 
(e.g., [[[['#,##0.00', '0.00%']], [['mm/dd/yyyy', 'General']]]]). Optional if 
formatsFile is provided. (JSON format)

options.name: --formats-file
options.description:
Path to a JSON file containing 2D array of format codes. 
Alternative to inline formats parameter.

options.name: --formulas
options.description:
2D array of formulas to set - include '=' prefix (e.g., 
[[[['=A1+B1', '=SUM(A:A)']], [['=C1*2', '=AVERAGE(B:B)']]]]). Optional if 
formulasFile is provided. (JSON format)

options.name: --formulas-file
options.description:
Path to a JSON file containing the formulas as a 2D array. 
Alternative to inline formulas parameter.

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --range-address
options.description:
Cell range address (e.g., 'A1', 'A1:D10', 'B:D') or named 
range name (e.g., 'SalesData') (required for: get-values, set-values, 
get-formulas, set-formulas, validate-formulas, clear-all, clear-contents, 
clear-formats, get-number-formats, set-number-format, set-number-formats, 
get-info)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description:
Name of the worksheet containing the range - REQUIRED for 
cell addresses, use empty string for named ranges only (required for: 
get-values, set-values, get-formulas, set-formulas, validate-formulas, 
clear-all, clear-contents, clear-formats, get-number-formats, set-number-format,
set-number-formats, get-used-range, get-current-region, get-info)

options.name: --source-range
options.description:
Source range address for copy operations (e.g., 'A1:D10') 
(required for: copy, copy-values, copy-formulas)

options.name: --source-sheet
options.description:
Source worksheet name for copy operations (required for: 
copy, copy-values, copy-formulas)

options.name: --target-range
options.description:
Target range address - can be single cell for paste 
destination (e.g., 'A1') (required for: copy, copy-values, 
copy-formulas)

options.name: --target-sheet
options.description:
Target worksheet name for copy operations (required for: 
copy, copy-values, copy-formulas)

options.name: --values
options.description:
2D array of values to set - rows are outer array, columns 
are inner array (e.g., [[[[1,2,3]],[[4,5,6]]]] for 2 rows x 3 cols). Optional if
valuesFile is provided. (JSON format)

options.name: --values-file
options.description:
Path to a JSON or CSV file containing the values. JSON: 2D 
array. CSV: rows/columns. Alternative to inline values parameter.

commands.description:
Core range operations: get/set values and formulas, copy 
ranges, clear content, and discover data regions. Use rangeedit for 
insert/delete/find/sort. Use rangeformat for styling/validation. Use rangelink 
for hyperlinks and cell protection. Calculation mode and explicit recalculation 
are handled by calculationmode. BEST PRACTICE: Use 'get-values' to check 
existing data before overwriting. Use 'clear-contents' (not 'clear-all') to 
preserve cell formatting when clearing data. set-values preserves existing 
formatting; use set-number-format after if format change needed. DATA FORMAT: 
values and formulas are 2D JSON arrays representing rows and columns. Example: 
[[[[row1col1, row1col2]], [[row2col1, row2col2]]]] Single cell returns 
[[[[value]]]] (always 2D). REQUIRED PARAMETERS: - sheetName + rangeAddress for 
cell operations (e.g., sheetName='Sheet1', rangeAddress='A1:D10') - For named 
ranges, use sheetName='' (empty string) and rangeAddress='MyNamedRange' COPY 
OPERATIONS: Specify source and target sheet/range for copy operations. NUMBER 
FORMATS: Use US locale format codes (e.g., '#,##0.00', 'mm/dd/yyyy', 
'0.00%').

commands.name: rangeedit

options.name: --delete-shift
options.description:
Direction to shift remaining cells: 'Up' or 'Left' 
(required for: delete-cells)

options.name: --find-options
options.description:
Search options: matchCase (default: false), matchEntireCell
(default: false), searchFormulas (default: true) (required for: 
find)

options.name: --find-value
options.description:
Text or value to search for (required for: 
replace)

options.name: --has-headers
options.description:
Whether the range has a header row to exclude from sorting 
(default: true)

options.name: --insert-shift
options.description:
Direction to shift existing cells: 'Down' or 'Right' 
(required for: insert-cells)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --range-address
options.description:
Cell range address where cells will be inserted (e.g., 
'A1:D10') (required)

options.name: --replace-options
options.description:
Replace options: matchCase (default: false), 
matchEntireCell (default: false), replaceAll (default: true) (required for: 
replace)

options.name: --replace-value
options.description:
Text or value to replace matches with (required for: 
replace)

options.name: --search-value
options.description:
Text or value to search for (required for: 
find)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description:
Name of the worksheet containing the range 
(required)

options.name: --sort-columns
options.description:
Array of sort specifications: [[{columnIndex: 1, ascending:
true}, ...]] - columnIndex is 1-based relative to range (required for: sort) 
(JSON format)

commands.description:
Range editing operations: insert/delete cells, rows, and 
columns; find/replace text; sort data. Use range for values/formulas/copy/clear 
operations. INSERT/DELETE CELLS: Specify shift direction to control how 
surrounding cells move. - Insert: 'Down' or 'Right' - Delete: 'Up' or 'Left' 
INSERT/DELETE ROWS: Use row range like '5:10' to insert/delete rows 5-10. 
INSERT/DELETE COLUMNS: Use column range like 'B:D' to insert/delete columns B-D.
FIND/REPLACE: Search within the specified range with optional case/cell 
matching. - Find returns up to 10 matching cell addresses with total count. - 
Replace modifies all matches by default. SORT: Specify sortColumns as array of 
{columnIndex: 1, ascending: true} objects. Column indices are 1-based relative 
to the range.

commands.name: rangeformat

options.name: --bold
options.description: Whether to apply bold formatting

options.name: --border-color
options.description: Border color as hex '#RRGGBB'

options.name: --border-style
options.description:
Border line style: 'continuous', 'dash', 'dot', 'dashdot', 
'dashdotdot', 'double', 'slantdashdot', 'none'

options.name: --border-weight
options.description:
Border weight: 'hairline', 'thin', 'medium', 
'thick'

options.name: --column-width
options.description:
Width in points (1 point = 1/72 inch, approx 0.35mm). 
Standard width ~8.43 points. Range: 0.25-409 points. (required for: 
set-column-width)

options.name: --error-message
options.description: Text for the error alert popup

options.name: --error-style
options.description:
Error alert style: 'stop' (prevents entry), 'warning' 
(allows override), 'information' (allows entry)

options.name: --error-title
options.description: Title for the error alert popup

options.name: --fill-color
options.description:
Cell fill (background) color as hex '#RRGGBB' (e.g., 
'#FFFF00' for yellow)

options.name: --font-color
options.description:
Font (foreground) color as hex '#RRGGBB' (e.g., '#FF0000' 
for red)

options.name: --font-name
options.description:
Font family name (e.g., 'Arial', 'Calibri', 'Times New 
Roman')

options.name: --font-size
options.description:
Font size in points (e.g., 10, 11, 12, 14, 
16)

options.name: --formula1
options.description:
First validation formula/value - for list validation use 
range '=$A$1:$A$10' or inline '"A,B,C"'

options.name: --formula2
options.description:
Second validation formula/value - required only for 
'between' and 'notBetween' operators

options.name: --horizontal-alignment
options.description:
Horizontal text alignment: 'left', 'center', 'right', 
'justify', 'fill'

options.name: --ignore-blank
options.description:
Whether to allow blank cells in validation (default: 
true)

options.name: --input-message
options.description: Text for the input message popup

options.name: --input-title
options.description: Title for the input message popup

options.name: --italic
options.description: Whether to apply italic formatting

options.name: --orientation
options.description:
Text rotation in degrees (-90 to 90, or 255 for 
vertical)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --range-address
options.description:
Cell range address (e.g., 'A1:D10') 
(required)

options.name: --row-height
options.description:
Height in points (1 point = 1/72 inch, approx 0.35mm). 
Default row height ~15 points. Range: 0-409 points. (required for: 
set-row-height)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description:
Name of the worksheet containing the range 
(required)

options.name: --show-dropdown
options.description:
Whether to show dropdown arrow for list validation 
(default: true)

options.name: --show-error-alert
options.description:
Whether to show error alert on invalid input (default: 
true)

options.name: --show-input-message
options.description:
Whether to show input message when cell is selected 
(default: false)

options.name: --style-name
options.description:
Built-in or custom style name (e.g., 'Heading 1', 'Good', 
'Bad', 'Currency', 'Percent'). Use 'Normal' to reset. (required for: 
set-style)

options.name: --underline
options.description: Whether to apply underline formatting

options.name: --validation-operator
options.description:
Validation comparison operator: 'between', 'notBetween', 
'equal', 'notEqual', 'greaterThan', 'lessThan', 'greaterThanOrEqual', 
'lessThanOrEqual'

options.name: --validation-type
options.description:
Data validation type: 'list', 'whole', 'decimal', 'date', 
'time', 'textLength', 'custom' (required for: validate-range)

options.name: --vertical-alignment
options.description:
Vertical text alignment: 'top', 'center' (or 'middle'), 
'bottom', 'justify'

options.name: --wrap-text
options.description: Whether to wrap text within cells

commands.description:
Range formatting operations: apply styles, set 
fonts/colors/borders, add data validation, merge cells, auto-fit dimensions. Use
range tool for values/formulas/copy/clear operations. set-style: Apply a named 
Excel style (Heading 1, Good, Bad, Neutral, Normal). Best for semantic status 
labels (Good/Bad/Neutral have fill colours and are theme-aware) and document 
hierarchy (Heading 1/2/3). NOTE: Heading styles do NOT apply a fill colour — use
format-range when you need a coloured header row. format-range: Apply any 
combination of bold, fillColor, fontColor, alignment, borders. Required whenever
you need a fill colour or custom branding. Pass ALL desired properties in a 
SINGLE call — do not call format-range multiple times for the same range. 
COLORS: Hex '#RRGGBB' (e.g., '#FF0000' for red, '#00FF00' for green) FONT: size 
in points (e.g., 12, 14, 16), alignment: 'left', 'center', 'right' / 'top', 
'middle', 'bottom' DATA VALIDATION: Restrict cell input with validation rules: -
Types: 'list', 'whole', 'decimal', 'date', 'time', 'textLength', 'custom' - For 
list validation, formula1 is the list source (e.g., '=$A$1:$A$10' or 
'"Option1,Option2,Option3"') - Operators: 'between', 'notBetween', 'equal', 
'notEqual', 'greaterThan', 'lessThan', 'greaterThanOrEqual', 'lessThanOrEqual' 
MERGE: Combines cells into one. Only top-left cell value is 
preserved.

commands.name: rangelink

options.name: --cell-address
options.description:
Single cell address (e.g., 'A1') (required for: 
add-hyperlink, get-hyperlink)

options.name: --display-text
options.description:
Text to display in the cell (optional, defaults to 
URL)

options.name: --locked
options.description:
Lock status: true = locked (protected when sheet protection
enabled), false = unlocked (editable) (required for: 
set-cell-lock)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --range-address
options.description:
Cell range address to remove hyperlinks from (e.g., 
'A1:D10') (required for: remove-hyperlink, set-cell-lock, 
get-cell-lock)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description: Name of the worksheet (required)

options.name: --tooltip
options.description: Tooltip text shown on hover (optional)

options.name: --url
options.description:
Hyperlink URL (web: 'https://...', file: 'file:///...', 
email: 'mailto:...') (required for: add-hyperlink)

commands.description:
Hyperlink and cell protection operations for Excel ranges. Use 
range for values/formulas, rangeformat for styling. HYPERLINKS: - 
'add-hyperlink': Add a clickable hyperlink to a cell (URL can be web, file, or 
mailto) - 'remove-hyperlink': Remove hyperlink(s) from cells while keeping the 
cell content - 'list-hyperlinks': Get all hyperlinks on a worksheet - 
'get-hyperlink': Get hyperlink details for a specific cell CELL PROTECTION: - 
'set-cell-lock': Lock or unlock cells (only effective when sheet protection is 
enabled) - 'get-cell-lock': Check if cells are locked Note: Cell locking only 
takes effect when the worksheet is protected.

commands.name: screenshot

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --quality
options.description:
Image quality: Medium (default, JPEG 75% scale), High (PNG 
full scale), Low (JPEG 50% scale)

options.name: --range-address
options.description: Range to capture (e.g., "A1:F20")

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description: Worksheet name (null for active sheet)

commands.description:
Capture Excel worksheet content as images for visual 
verification. Uses Excel's built-in rendering (CopyPicture) to capture ranges as
PNG images. Captures formatting, conditional formatting, charts, and all visual 
elements. ACTIONS: - capture: Capture a specific range as an image - 
capture-sheet: Capture the entire used area of a worksheet RETURNS: 
Base64-encoded image data with dimensions metadata. For MCP: returned as native 
ImageContent (no file handling needed). For CLI: use --output <path> to 
save the image directly to a PNG/JPEG file instead of returning base64 inline. 
Quality defaults to Medium (JPEG 75% scale) which is 4-8x smaller than High 
(PNG). Use High only when fine detail inspection is needed.

commands.name: sheet

options.name: --after-sheet
options.description: Optional: Name of sheet to position after

options.name: --before-sheet
options.description: Optional: Name of sheet to position before

options.name: --file-path
options.description:
Optional file path when batch contains multiple workbooks. 
If omitted, uses primary workbook.

options.name: --new-name
options.description:
New name for the worksheet (required for: 
rename)

options.name: --old-name
options.description:
Current name of the worksheet (required for: 
rename)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description:
Name for the new worksheet (required for: create, delete, 
move)

options.name: --source-file
options.description:
Full path to the source workbook (required for: 
copy-to-file, move-to-file)

options.name: --source-name
options.description:
Name of the source worksheet (required for: 
copy)

options.name: --source-sheet
options.description:
Name of the sheet to copy (required for: copy-to-file, 
move-to-file)

options.name: --target-file
options.description:
Full path to the target workbook (required for: 
copy-to-file, move-to-file)

options.name: --target-name
options.description:
Name for the copied worksheet (required for: 
copy)

options.name: --target-sheet-name
options.description:
Optional: New name for the copied sheet (default: keeps 
original name)

commands.description:
Worksheet lifecycle management: create, rename, copy, delete, 
move, list sheets. Use range for data operations. Use sheetstyle for tab colors 
and visibility. ATOMIC OPERATIONS: 'copy-to-file' and 'move-to-file' don't 
require a session - they open/close files automatically. POSITIONING: For 
'move', 'copy-to-file', 'move-to-file' - use 'before' OR 'after' (not both) to 
position the sheet relative to another. If neither specified, moves to end. 
NOTE: MCP tool is manually implemented in ExcelWorksheetTool.cs to properly 
handle mixed session requirements (copy-to-file and move-to-file are atomic and 
don't need sessions).

commands.name: slicer

options.name: --clear-first
options.description:
If true, clears existing selection before setting new items
(default: true)

options.name: --column-name
options.description:
Name of the column to use for the slicer (required for: 
create-table-slicer)

options.name: --destination-sheet
options.description:
Worksheet where slicer will be placed (required for: 
create-slicer, create-table-slicer)

options.name: --field-name
options.description:
Name of the field to use for the slicer (required for: 
create-slicer)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --pivot-table-name
options.description:
Name of the PivotTable to create slicer for (required for: 
create-slicer)

options.name: --position
options.description:
Top-left cell position for the slicer (e.g., "H2") 
(required for: create-slicer, create-table-slicer)

options.name: --selected-items
options.description:
Items to select (show in PivotTable) (required for: 
set-slicer-selection, set-table-slicer-selection) (JSON format)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --slicer-name
options.description:
Name for the new slicer (required for: create-slicer, 
set-slicer-selection, delete-slicer, create-table-slicer, 
set-table-slicer-selection, delete-table-slicer)

options.name: --table-name
options.description:
Name of the Excel Table (required for: 
create-table-slicer)

commands.description:
Slicer visual filters for PivotTables and Excel Tables. 
PIVOTTABLE SLICERS: create-slicer, list-slicers, set-slicer-selection, 
delete-slicer. TABLE SLICERS: create-table-slicer, list-table-slicers, 
set-table-slicer-selection, delete-table-slicer. NAMING: Auto-generate 
descriptive names like {FieldName}Slicer (e.g., RegionSlicer). SELECTION: 
selectedItems as list of strings. Empty list clears filter (shows all items). 
Set clearFirst=false to add to existing selection.

commands.name: table

options.name: --column-name
options.description:
Name of the column to set total function on (required for: 
set-column-total)

options.name: --dax-query
options.description:
DAX EVALUATE query (e.g., 'EVALUATE Sales' or 'EVALUATE 
SUMMARIZE(...)') (required for: create-from-dax, update-dax)

options.name: --has-headers
options.description:
True if first row contains column headers (default: 
true)

options.name: --new-name
options.description:
New name for the table (must be unique in workbook) 
(required for: rename)

options.name: --new-range
options.description:
New range address (e.g., 'A1:F20') (required for: 
resize)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --range-address
options.description:
Cell range address for the table (e.g., 'A1:D10') (required
for: create)

options.name: --rows
options.description:
2D array of row data to append - column order must match 
table columns. Optional if rowsFile is provided. (JSON format)

options.name: --rows-file
options.description:
Path to a JSON or CSV file containing the rows to append. 
JSON: 2D array. CSV: rows/columns. Alternative to inline rows 
parameter.

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description:
Name of the worksheet to create the table on (required for:
create, create-from-dax)

options.name: --show-totals
options.description:
True to show totals row, false to hide (required for: 
toggle-totals)

options.name: --strip-bracket-column-names
options.description:
When true, renames source table columns that contain 
literal bracket characters (removes brackets) before adding to the Data Model. 
This modifies the Excel table column headers in the worksheet.

options.name: --table-name
options.description:
Name for the new table (must be unique in workbook) 
(required for: create, rename, delete, read, resize, toggle-totals, 
set-column-total, append, get-data, set-style, add-to-data-model, 
create-from-dax, update-dax, get-dax)

options.name: --table-style
options.description:
Table style name (e.g., 'TableStyleMedium2', 
'TableStyleLight1'). Optional. (required for: set-style)

options.name: --target-cell
options.description:
Target cell address for table placement (default: 
'A1')

options.name: --total-function
options.description:
Totals function name: Sum, Count, Average, Min, Max, 
CountNums, StdDev, Var, None (required for: set-column-total)

options.name: --visible-only
options.description:
True to return only visible (non-filtered) rows; false for 
all rows (default: false)

commands.description:
Excel Tables (ListObjects) - lifecycle and data operations. 
Tables provide structured references, automatic formatting, and Data Model 
integration. BEST PRACTICE: Use 'list' to check existing tables before creating.
Prefer 'append'/'resize'/'rename' over delete+recreate to preserve references. 
WARNING: Deleting tables used as PivotTable sources or in Data Model 
relationships will break those objects. DATA MODEL WORKFLOW: To analyze 
worksheet data with DAX/Power Pivot: 1. Create or identify an Excel Table on a 
worksheet 2. Use 'add-to-datamodel' to add the table to Power Pivot 3. Then use 
datamodel to create DAX measures on it DAX-BACKED TABLES: Create tables 
populated by DAX EVALUATE queries: - 'create-from-dax': Create a new table 
backed by a DAX query (e.g., SUMMARIZE, FILTER) - 'update-dax': Update the DAX 
query for an existing DAX-backed table - 'get-dax': Get the DAX query info for a
table (check if it's DAX-backed) Related: tablecolumn (filter/sort/columns), 
datamodel (DAX measures, evaluate queries)

commands.name: tablecolumn

options.name: --ascending
options.description:
Sort order: true = ascending (A-Z, 0-9), false = descending
(default: true)

options.name: --column-name
options.description:
Name of the column to filter (required for: apply-filter, 
apply-filter-values, add-column, remove-column, sort, get-column-number-format, 
set-column-number-format)

options.name: --criteria
options.description:
Filter criteria string (e.g., '>100', '=Active', 
'<>Closed') (required for: apply-filter)

options.name: --format-code
options.description:
Number format code in US locale (e.g., '#,##0.00', '0%', 
'yyyy-mm-dd') (required for: set-column-number-format)

options.name: --new-name
options.description: New column name (required for: rename-column)

options.name: --old-name
options.description:
Current column name (required for: 
rename-column)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --position
options.description:
1-based column position (optional, defaults to end of 
table)

options.name: --region
options.description:
Table region: 'Data', 'Headers', 'Totals', or 'All' 
(required for: get-structured-reference)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sort-columns
options.description:
List of sort specifications: [[{columnName: 'Col1', 
ascending: true}, ...]] - applied in order (required for: sort-multi) (JSON 
format)

options.name: --table-name
options.description: Name of the Excel table (required)

options.name: --values
options.description:
List of exact values to include in the filter (required 
for: apply-filter-values) (JSON format)

commands.description:
Table column, filtering, and sorting operations for Excel 
Tables (ListObjects). Use table for table-level lifecycle and data operations. 
FILTERING: - 'apply-filter': Simple criteria filter (e.g., ">100", "=Active",
"<>Closed") - 'apply-filter-values': Filter by exact values (provide list 
of values to include) - 'clear-filters': Remove all active filters - 
'get-filters': See current filter state SORTING: - 'sort': Single column sort 
(ascending/descending) - 'sort-multi': Multi-column sort (provide list of 
{columnName, ascending} objects) COLUMN MANAGEMENT: - 
'add-column'/'remove-column'/'rename-column': Modify table structure NUMBER 
FORMATS: Use US locale format codes (e.g., '#,##0.00', '0%', 
'yyyy-mm-dd')

commands.name: vba

options.name: --module-name
options.description:
Name of the VBA module (required for: view, import, update,
delete)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --parameters
options.description:
Optional parameters to pass to the procedure (required for:
run)

options.name: --procedure-name
options.description:
Name of the procedure to run (e.g., "Module1.MySub") 
(required for: run)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Optional timeout for execution

options.name: --vba-code
options.description:
VBA code to import (required for: import, 
update)

options.name: --vba-code-file
options.description: Path to file containing vbaCode

commands.description:
VBA scripts (requires .xlsm and VBA trust enabled). 
PREREQUISITES: - Workbook must be macro-enabled (.xlsm) - VBA trust must be 
enabled for automation RUN: procedureName format is 'Module.Procedure' (e.g., 
'Module1.MySub').

commands.name: window

options.name: --height
options.description: Window height in points

options.name: --left
options.description: Window left position in points

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --preset
options.description:
Preset name: 'left-half', 'right-half', 'top-half', 
'bottom-half', 'center', 'full-screen' (required for: arrange)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --text
options.description:
Status bar text to display (e.g. "Building PivotTable from 
Sales data...") (required for: set-status-bar)

options.name: --top
options.description: Window top position in points

options.name: --width
options.description: Window width in points

options.name: --window-state
options.description:
Window state: 'normal', 'minimized', or 'maximized' 
(required for: set-state)

commands.description:
Control Excel window visibility, position, state, and status 
bar. Use to show/hide Excel, bring it to front, reposition, or 
maximize/minimize. Set status bar text to give users real-time feedback during 
operations. VISIBILITY: 'show' makes Excel visible AND brings to front. 'hide' 
hides Excel. Visibility changes are reflected in session metadata (session list 
shows updated state). WINDOW STATE values: 'normal', 'minimized', 'maximized'. 
ARRANGE presets: 'left-half', 'right-half', 'top-half', 'bottom-half', 'center',
'full-screen'. STATUS BAR: 'set-status-bar' displays text in Excel's status bar.
'clear-status-bar' restores default.

commands.name: worksheetstyle

options.name: --blue
options.description:
Blue color component (0-255) (required for: 
set-tab-color)

options.name: --green
options.description:
Green color component (0-255) (required for: 
set-tab-color)

options.name: --output
options.description:
Write output to file instead of stdout. For image results, 
decodes and saves as binary file.

options.name: --red
options.description:
Red color component (0-255) (required for: 
set-tab-color)

options.name: --session
options.description: Session ID from 'session open' command

options.name: --sheet-name
options.description: Name of the worksheet to color (required)

options.name: --visibility
options.description:
Visibility level: 'visible', 'hidden', or 'veryhidden' 
(required for: set-visibility)

commands.description:
Worksheet styling operations for tab colors and visibility. Use
sheet for lifecycle operations (create, rename, copy, delete, move). TAB COLORS:
Use RGB values (0-255 each) to set custom tab colors for visual organization. 
VISIBILITY LEVELS: - 'visible': Normal visible sheet - 'hidden': Hidden but 
accessible via Format > Sheet > Unhide - 'veryhidden': Only accessible via
VBA (protection against casual unhiding)
